title: sbpannoni/wf-ion-CoV2
description: SARS-CoV-2 consensus and variant calling from Ion Torrent Ampliseq GX BAM files.

input.title: Input Options
input.description: Parameters for finding and handling input data for analysis.

bam.title: BAM
bam.description: Ion Torrent BAM file(s) to use in the analysis.
bam.help_text: Accepts (i) a single BAM file, (ii) a directory of BAM files, or (iii) a directory of per-sample sub-directories each containing BAM files. BAMs may be pre-aligned (from Torrent Suite / Ion Reporter) or unaligned; the pipeline detects the alignment state and re-aligns to MN908947.3 with minimap2 when needed.

analyse_unclassified.description: Analyse unclassified reads from input directory. By default the workflow will not process reads in the unclassified directory.
analyse_unclassified.help_text: If selected and if the input is a multiplex directory the workflow will also process the unclassified directory.

primer_scheme_selection.title: Primer Scheme Selection
primer_scheme_selection.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

scheme_name.help_text: This should be set to `SARS-CoV-2` or your custom scheme name. This affects the choice of scheme versions available.

scheme_version.help_text: Primer scheme version. For Ion Torrent Ampliseq GX data use Ampliseq-GX/V1. Place the primer BED file from Ion Reporter at data/primer_schemes/SARS-CoV-2/Ampliseq-GX/V1/SARS-CoV-2.scheme.bed before running.

custom_scheme.help_text: If you have a custom primer scheme you can enter the details here. This must be the full path to the directory containing your appropriately named scheme bed and fasta files; <SCHEME_NAME>.bed and <SCHEME_NAME>.fasta. More details [here](https://labs.epi2me.io/ont-midnight-scheme-update/).

samples.title: Sample Options
samples.description: Parameters that relate to samples such as sample sheets and sample names.

sample_sheet.description: A CSV file used to map barcodes to sample aliases. The sample sheet can be provided when the input data is a directory containing sub-directories with FASTQ files.
sample_sheet.help_text: The sample sheet is a CSV file with, minimally, columns named `barcode` and `alias`. Extra columns are allowed. A `type` column is required for certain workflows and should have the following values; `test_sample`, `positive_control`, `negative_control`, `no_template_control`.

sample.description: A single sample name for non-multiplexed data. Permissible if passing a single .fastq(.gz) file or directory of .fastq(.gz) files.

output.title: Output Options
output.description: Parameters for saving and naming workflow outputs.

out_dir.description: Directory for output of all workflow results.

report_depth.description: Min. depth for percentage coverage. (e.g. 89% genome covered at > `report_depth`)

report_clade.description: Show results of Nextclade analysis in report.

report_coverage.description: Show genome coverage traces in report.

report_lineage.description: Show results of Pangolin analysis in report.

advanced_options.title: Advanced Options
advanced_options.description: Advanced options for configuring processes inside the workflow.
advanced_options.help_text: These advanced options do not need to be changed for typical use, but allow fine tuning of workflows for users who want more control over the workflow.

artic_threads.description: Number of CPU threads to use per artic task.
artic_threads.help_text: The total CPU resource used by the workflow is constrained by the executor configuration.

pangolin_threads.description: Number of CPU threads to use per pangolin task.
pangolin_threads.help_text: The total CPU resource used by the workflow is constrained by the executor configuration.

genotype_variants.description: Report genotyping information for scheme's known variants of interest, optionally provide file path as argument.

list_schemes.description: List primer schemes and exit without running analysis.

ivar_min_trim_len.title: iVar minimum trimmed read length
ivar_min_trim_len.description: Minimum read length after primer trimming (iVar -m). Reads shorter than this are discarded.

ivar_min_qual.title: iVar minimum base quality
ivar_min_qual.description: Minimum base quality score (Phred) for pileup and variant calling (iVar -q).

ivar_min_freq.title: iVar minimum allele frequency
ivar_min_freq.description: Minimum allele frequency to call a variant (iVar -t). Set lower (e.g. 0.03) for low-frequency variant detection.

ivar_min_depth.title: iVar minimum depth
ivar_min_depth.description: Minimum read depth to call a variant or include a position in the consensus (iVar -m).

ivar_consensus_freq.title: iVar consensus frequency threshold
ivar_consensus_freq.description: Frequency threshold for consensus base calling (iVar -t). 0.0 uses the most frequent base; 0.5 requires >50% support.

update_data.description: Update Pangolin and Nextclade data at runtime.

pangolin_options.description: Pass options to Pangolin, for example "--analysis-mode fast --min-length 26000".

nextclade_data_tag.description: The tag of the nextclade data packet

testkit.title: Test kit identifier
testkit.description: Test kit identifier, used in reporting.

disable_ping.description: Enable to prevent sending a workflow ping.
